title: acer cissifolium subsp. henryi
intro: Acer cissifolium subsp. henryi, also known as the Henry's maple or the Chinese maple, is a subspecies of deciduous shrub or small tree native to the mountainous regions of China. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Acer cissifolium subsp. henryi include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce cholesterol levels, and alleviate symptoms of insomnia and anxiety. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves, bark, and roots are typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are used to support cardiovascular health and promote relaxation.
